perturbation.original_text: 20. Independent Contractor. Company has engaged North for certain rights which are set forth in this Agreement, and North's relationship to Company, and Company's relationship to North, shall at all times be that of an independent contractor.
perturbation.changed_text: 20. Independent Contractor. Company has engaged North for certain rights which are set forth in this Agreement, and North's relationship to Company, and Company's relationship to North, shall at all times be that of an independent contractor. However, for tax purposes, North shall be treated as an employee.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that North is an independent contractor but will be treated as an employee for tax purposes, the contract creates a contradiction. The IRS has specific criteria for determining whether someone is an independent contractor or an employee, and treating someone as both simultaneously introduces legal uncertainty and conflicts with tax regulations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: IRS guidelines on independent contractor vs. employee classification
perturbation.law_explanation: The IRS defines an employee based on behavioral control, financial control, and the relationship of the parties. The modified text contradicts this by stating that North is an independent contractor in the main clause but will be treated as an employee for tax purposes. This could lead to misclassification and tax evasion issues, violating the established legal definitions.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Independent contractor defined | Internal Revenue Service Skip to main content Independent contractor defined English Español 中文 (繁體) 한국어 Русский Tiếng Việt More In File Individuals Businesses and self-employed Business tax account Employer ID numbers Business taxes Business structures Operating a business Self-employed Independent contractors Gig workers Small business Large business Charities and nonprofits International taxpayers Governmental liaisons Federal, state and local governments Indian tribal governments Tax exempt bonds Taxpayer identification numbers (TIN) People such as doctors, dentists, veterinarians, lawyers, accountants, contractors, subcontractors, public stenographers, or auctioneers who are in an independent trade, business, or profession in which they offer their services to the general public are generally independent contractors. However, whether these people are independent contractors or employees depends on the facts in each case. The general rule is that an individual is an independent contractor if the person for whom the services are performed has the right to control or direct only the result of the work and not what will be done and how it will be done. If you are an independent contractor, then you are self-employed. The earnings of a person who is working as an independent contractor are subject to self-employment tax . To find out what your tax obligations are, visit the Self-Employed Individuals Tax Center . Generally, the person for whom the services are performed must report payments to independent contractors on Form 1099-NEC, Nonemployee Compensation , in box 1. You are not an independent contractor if you perform services that can be controlled by an employer (what will be done and how it will be done). This applies even if you are given freedom of action. What matters is that the employer has the legal right to control the details of how the services are performed. If an employer-employee relationship exists (regardless of what the relationship is called), then you are not an independent contractor and your earnings are generally not subject to self-employment tax. However, your earnings as an employee may be subject to FICA (social security and Medicare tax) and income tax withholding. Generally, earnings of an employee are reported on Form W-2, Wage and Tax Statement . Refer to the page, Independent Contractor (Self-Employed) or Employee? or Publication 15-A, Employer’s Supplement Tax Guide , for more information. Related Topics Reporting payments to independent contractor Estimated taxes Backup withholding Topic no. 307, Backup withholding Page Last Reviewed or Updated: 02-Jun-2025 Share Facebook Twitter Linkedin Print
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction because it designates North as an independent contractor while simultaneously stating they will be treated as an employee for tax purposes. The IRS snippet defines an independent contractor as someone who offers their services to the general public and whose work is controlled only in terms of the result, not the means. Treating North as an employee for tax purposes suggests the company exerts a level of control inconsistent with independent contractor status, thus contradicting the initial classification and creating a potential misclassification under IRS guidelines, as indicated by the Law Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 22. Governing Law. The Agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of the State of Florida in the United States of America, and venue for any such legal action shall be in the Circuit Court or County Court in Orlando, FL or the U.S. District Court having jurisdiction over Orlando, FL.
perturbation.changed_text: 22. Governing Law. The Agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of the State of Florida, except for matters pertaining to intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China, and venue for any such legal action shall be in the Circuit Court or County Court in Orlando, FL or the U.S. District Court having jurisdiction over Orlando, FL.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces a conflict of laws issue. While the contract states it's governed by Florida law, it carves out intellectual property matters to be governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China. This creates uncertainty as to which jurisdiction's laws apply in disputes involving intellectual property, potentially leading to enforcement challenges and contradicting established principles of contract law and conflict of laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws provides guidelines on which jurisdiction's laws should govern a contract. While parties can choose the governing law, the choice is not absolute and must bear a reasonable relationship to the contract. Applying Chinese law to intellectual property matters in a Florida-governed contract creates uncertainty and may be unenforceable if it contradicts fundamental principles of Florida or US law.
perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces a potential legal contradiction by specifying that the laws of the People's Republic of China govern intellectual property matters, while the rest of the agreement is governed by Florida law. This creates uncertainty and conflict, contradicting established principles of contract law, as highlighted by the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation. Although there are no scraped law snippets available, the conflict created introduces ambiguity in the governing law, which falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 27. Compliance with Laws. Company represents and warrants to North that it will comply with all rules, regulations, laws, orders, decrees and ordinances of the United States, each state and political subdivision thereof, and each and every foreign country having legal jurisdiction over any aspect of Company's activities under this Agreement, including, but not limited to, the use of North's Likeness in advertisements for and on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 27. Compliance with Laws. Company represents and warrants to North that it will comply with all rules, regulations, laws, orders, decrees and ordinances of the United States, each state and political subdivision thereof, and each and every foreign country having legal jurisdiction over any aspect of Company's activities under this Agreement, except for laws related to consumer protection in international markets, including, but not limited to, the use of North's Likeness in advertisements for and on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By explicitly excluding compliance with consumer protection laws in international markets, the company creates a clause that is in direct contradiction with existing consumer protection laws. This attempts to waive the company's legal duties, especially advertising regulations, which are considered illegal and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Varying international consumer protection laws (e.g., EU Consumer Rights Directive)
perturbation.law_explanation: The EU Consumer Rights Directive, for example, establishes minimum consumer protection standards across the European Union. The modification of the contract, attempting to circumvent compliance with similar international regulations on advertising and consumer rights, will be considered void and unenforceable, due to attempts to waive consumer protection, or engaging in false advertising.

Use the Advanced search Document 32011L0083 Help Print ​ Directive 2011/83/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council of 25 October 2011 on consumer rights, amending Council Directive 93/13/EEC and Directive 1999/44/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council and repealing Council Directive 85/577/EEC and Directive 97/7/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council Text with EEA relevance Directive 2011/83/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council of 25 October 2011 on consumer rights, amending Council Directive 93/13/EEC and Directive 1999/44/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council and repealing Council Directive 85/577/EEC and Directive 97/7/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council Text with EEA relevance Directive 2011/83/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council of 25 October 2011 on consumer rights, amending Council Directive 93/13/EEC and Directive 1999/44/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council and repealing Council Directive 85/577/EEC and Directive 97/7/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council Text with EEA relevance OJ L 304, 22.11.2011, p. 64–88 (BG, ES, CS, DA, DE, ET, EL, EN, FR, IT, LV, LT, HU, MT, NL, PL, PT, RO, SK, SL, FI, SV) This document has been published in a special edition(s) (HR) ⏵ This document has been published in a special edition(s) (HR) Special edition in Croatian: Chapter 15 Volume 00
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes compliance with consumer protection laws in international markets, which directly contradicts the principle that businesses must adhere to consumer rights regulations, exemplified by the EU Consumer Rights Directive (Directive 2011/83/EU), which aims to establish minimum consumer protection standards across the European Union. The original contract language required compliance with all laws, rules, regulations, orders, decrees, and ordinances. The revised language creates an exception for consumer protection in international markets, and violates the consumer rights standards by attempting to waive these rights.
